heading: Research and training centers; development or expansion
label: 285c–5 Research and training centers; development or expansion
notes: Amendments1993—Subsecs. (d), (e). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 103–43
amendments: Amendments1993—Subsecs. (d), (e). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 103–43

children.heading: Diabetes mellitus and related endocrine and metabolic diseases
children.label: a Diabetes mellitus and related endocrine and metabolic diseases
A
children.text: utilize the facilities of a single institution, or be formed from a consortium of cooperating institutions, meeting such research and training qualifications as may be prescribed by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: research in the diagnosis and treatment of diabetes mellitus and related endocrine and metabolic diseases and the complications resulting from such diseases;

children.label: ii
children.text: training programs for physicians and allied health personnel in current methods of diagnosis and treatment of such diseases and complications, and in research in diabetes; and

children.label: iii
children.text: information programs for physicians and allied health personnel who provide primary care for patients with such diseases or complications.
A center may use funds provided under paragraph (1) to provide stipends for nurses and allied health professionals enrolled in research training programs described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Digestive diseases and related functional, congenital, metabolic disorders, and normal development of digestive tract
children.label: b Digestive diseases and related functional, congenital, metabolic disorders, and normal development of digestive tract

children.text: shall utilize the facilities of a single institution, or be formed from a consortium of cooperating institutions, meeting such research qualifications as may be prescribed by the Secretary;

shall develop and conduct basic and clinical research into the cause, diagnosis, early detection, prevention, control, and treatment of digestive diseases and nutritional disorders and related functional, congenital, or metabolic complications resulting from such diseases or disorders;

children.label: A
children.text: providing information for patients with such diseases and the families of such patients, physicians and others who care for such patients, and the general public;

children.label: B
children.text: model programs for cost effective and preventive patient care; and

children.label: C
children.text: training physicians and scientists in research on such diseases, disorders, and complications; and

may perform research and participate in epidemiological studies and data collection relevant to digestive diseases and disorders and disseminate such research, studies, and data to the health care profession and to the public.
shall utilize the facilities of a single institution, or be formed from a consortium of cooperating institutions, meeting such research qualifications as may be prescribed by the Secretary;
shall develop and conduct basic and clinical research into the cause, diagnosis, early detection, prevention, control, and treatment of kidney and urologic diseases;

children.label: A
children.text: providing information for patients with such diseases, disorders, and complications and the families of such patients, physicians and others who care for such patients, and the general public;

children.label: B
children.text: model programs for cost effective and preventive patient care; and

children.label: C
children.text: training physicians and scientists in research on such diseases; and

may perform research and participate in epidemiological studies and data collection relevant to kidney and urologic diseases in order to disseminate such research, studies, and data to the health care profession and to the public.

The Director of the Institute shall, subject to the extent of amounts made available in appropriations Acts, provide for the development or substantial expansion of centers for research and training regarding nutritional disorders, including obesity.

The Director of the Institute shall carry out paragraph (1) in collaboration with the Director of the National Cancer Institute and with the Directors of such other agencies of the National Institutes of Health as the Director of NIH determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: utilize the facilities of a single institution, or be formed from a consortium of cooperating institutions, meeting such research and training qualifications as may be prescribed by the Director;

children.label: B
children.text: conduct basic and clinical research into the cause, diagnosis, early detection, prevention, control and treatment of nutritional disorders, including obesity and the impact of nutrition and diet on child development;

children.label: C
children.text: conduct training programs for physicians and allied health professionals in current methods of diagnosis and treatment of such diseases and complications, and in research in such disorders; and

children.label: D
children.text: conduct information programs for physicians and allied health professionals who provide primary care for patients with such disorders or complications.

children.heading: Geographic distribution; period of support, additional periods
children.label: e Geographic distribution; period of support, additional periods
children.text: Insofar as practicable, centers developed or expanded under this section should be geographically dispersed throughout the United States and in environments with proven research capabilities. Support of a center under this section may be for a period of not to exceed five years and such period may be extended by the Director of the Institute for additional periods of not more than five years each if the operations of such center have been reviewed by an appropriate technical and scientific peer review group established by the Director and if such group has recommended to the Director that such period should be extended.
